input.background: Consider an investigation into the possible effects on non-smokers of allowing smoking in a shared office environment. The investigators measured lung capacity using different methods and scenarios to identify differences between the central tendency of the samples collected. The tests explored in this chapter include: $t$ test, Mann-Whitney $U$ test, paired $t$ test, and Wilcoxon matched-pairs test.
input.question: Measured the lung capacity on a scale of 1 to 5 (where 1 was poor and 5 was excellent) of non-smoking staff in an office where smoking was allowed and similarly on staff in an adjacent office where smoking had been banned for a month. Which test would you employ to identify differences between the central tendency of the samples?

output.answer: A Mann-Whitney $U$ test would be appropriate for this unpaired comparison of ranked scores.

meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
